message:
feat: configure sync.remote for contributor fork workflows

When bd init --contributor detects a fork setup (upstream remote exists),
it now configures sync.remote = upstream. This ensures bd sync pulls
beads from the source repo (upstream/main) rather than the fork's
potentially outdated origin/main.

Changes:
- Add sync.remote config in contributor wizard when fork detected
- Modify doSyncFromMain() to use configured sync.remote
- Add getDefaultBranchForRemote() to support any remote name
- Verify configured remote exists before fetching

Fixes bd-bx9

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -30,7 +30,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-bhd","title":"Git history fallback assumes .beads is direct child of repo root","description":"## Problem

`checkGitHistoryForDeletions` assumes the repo structure:

```go
repoRoot := filepath.Dir(beadsDir)  // Assumes .beads is in repo root
jsonlPath := filepath.Join(".beads", "beads.jsonl")
```

But `.beads` could be in a subdirectory (monorepo, nested project), and the actual JSONL filename could be different (configured via `metadata.json`).

## Location
`internal/importer/importer.go:865-869`

## Impact
- Git search will fail silently for repos with non-standard structure
- Monorepo users won't get deletion propagation

## Fix
1. Use `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` to find actual repo root
2. Compute relative path from repo root to JSONL
3. Or use `git -C <dir>` to run from beadsDir directly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-25T12:51:03.46856-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:05:40.754716-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:05:40.754716-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-bok","title":"bd doctor --fix needs non-interactive mode (-y/--yes flag)","description":"When running `bd doctor --fix` in non-interactive mode (scripts, CI, Claude Code), it prompts 'Continue? (Y/n):' and fails with EOF.

**Expected**: A `-y` or `--yes` flag to auto-confirm fixes.

**Workaround**: Currently have to run `bd init` instead, but that's not discoverable from the doctor output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-27T20:21:10.290649-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T22:17:12.607642-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T21:56:14.708313-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-bt6y","title":"Improve compact/daemon/merge documentation and UX","description":"Multiple documentation and UX issues encountered:
1. "bd compact --analyze" fails with misleading "requires SQLite storage" error when daemon is running. Needs --no-daemon or better error.
2. "bd merge" help text is outdated (refers to 3-way merge instead of issue merging).
3. Daemon mode purpose isn't clear to local-only users.
4. Compact/cleanup commands are hard to discover.

Proposed fixes:
- Fix compact+daemon interaction or error message.
- Update "bd merge" help text.
- Add "when to use daemon" section to docs.
- Add maintenance section to quickstart.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-20T18:55:43.637047-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-29T22:06:06.330457-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T23:10:43.884784-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-bx9","title":"bd init --contributor should configure sync.remote=upstream for fork workflows","description":"When running `bd init --contributor` in a fork workflow (where `upstream` remote points to the original repo), the wizard should configure beads to sync from `upstream/main` rather than `origin/main`.

**Current behavior:**
- Contributor mode detects the fork setup (upstream remote exists)
- Sets up planning repo and auto-routing
- Does NOT configure sync remote
- `bd sync` on feature branches shows "No upstream configured, using --from-main mode" and syncs from `origin/main`

**Expected behavior:**
- Contributor mode should also set `sync.remote = upstream` (or similar config)
- `bd sync` should pull beads from `upstream/main` (source of truth)

**Why this matters:**
- The fork's `origin/main` may be behind `upstream/main`
- Contributors want the latest issues from the source repo
- Code PRs go: local -> origin -> upstream, but beads should come FROM upstream

**Suggested fix:**
Add to `runContributorWizard()` after detecting fork:
```go
if isFork {
    store.SetConfig(ctx, "sync.remote", "upstream")
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-29T00:39:05.137488727-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-29T00:39:05.137488727-05:00","labels":["contributor","sync"]}
+{"id":"bd-bx9","title":"bd init --contributor should configure sync.remote=upstream for fork workflows","description":"When running `bd init --contributor` in a fork workflow (where `upstream` remote points to the original repo), the wizard should configure beads to sync from `upstream/main` rather than `origin/main`.

**Current behavior:**
- Contributor mode detects the fork setup (upstream remote exists)
- Sets up planning repo and auto-routing
- Does NOT configure sync remote
- `bd sync` on feature branches shows "No upstream configured, using --from-main mode" and syncs from `origin/main`

**Expected behavior:**
- Contributor mode should also set `sync.remote = upstream` (or similar config)
- `bd sync` should pull beads from `upstream/main` (source of truth)

**Why this matters:**
- The fork's `origin/main` may be behind `upstream/main`
- Contributors want the latest issues from the source repo
- Code PRs go: local -> origin -> upstream, but beads should come FROM upstream

**Suggested fix:**
Add to `runContributorWizard()` after detecting fork:
```go
if isFork {
    store.SetConfig(ctx, "sync.remote", "upstream")
}
```","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-29T00:39:05.137488727-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-29T23:22:53.036271-08:00","labels":["contributor","sync"]}
 {"id":"bd-c362","title":"Extract database search logic into helper function","description":"The logic for finding a database in a beads directory is duplicated:
- FindDatabasePath() BEADS_DIR section (beads.go:141-169)
- findDatabaseInTree() (beads.go:248-280)

Both implement the same search order:
1. Check config.json first (single source of truth)
2. Fall back to canonical beads.db
3. Search for *.db files, filtering backups and vc.db

Refactoring suggestion:
Extract to a helper function like:
  func findDatabaseInBeadsDir(beadsDir string) string

Benefits:
- Single source of truth for database search logic
- Easier to maintain and update search order
- Reduces code duplication

Related to [deleted:bd-e16b] implementation.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-11-02T18:34:02.831543-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T22:27:33.794656-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T22:27:33.794656-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-c4rq","title":"Refactor: Move staleness check inside daemon branch","description":"## Problem

Currently ensureDatabaseFresh() is called before the daemon mode check, but it checks daemonClient != nil internally and returns early. This is redundant.

**Location:** All read commands (list.go:196, show.go:27, ready.go:102, status.go:80, etc.)

## Current Pattern

Call happens before daemon check, function checks daemonClient internally.

## Better Pattern

Move staleness check to direct mode branch only, after daemon check.

## Impact
Low - minor performance improvement (avoids one function call per command in daemon mode)

## Effort
Medium - requires refactoring 8 command files

## Priority
Low - can defer to future cleanup PR","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-11-20T20:17:45.119583-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-29T22:06:06.330716-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T23:37:52.276192-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-c8x","title":"Don't search parent directories for .beads databases","description":"bd currently walks up the directory tree looking for .beads directories, which can find unrelated databases (e.g., ~/.beads). This causes confusing warnings and potential data pollution.

Should either:
1. Stop at git root (don't search above it)
2. Only use explicit BEADS_DB env var or local .beads
3. At minimum, don't search in home directory","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-27T22:10:41.992686-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T22:17:12.607956-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T22:15:55.878353-08:00"}
